sourceMovie.title: Hollow Man
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Hollow Man for the same reason it broke through in 2000: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a science fiction rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Paul Verhoeven keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The action and science fiction blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: RoboCop
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Hollow Man clicked for you, RoboCop (1987) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and science fiction, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Hollow Man?
faq.answer: Try RoboCop, Upgrade, Resident Evil—each shares DNA with Hollow Man while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Hollow Man?
faq.answer: Hollow Man (2000) is best described as Action, Science Fiction, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Hollow Man part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Hollow Man Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
